hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Abd Al-Rahman ibn Al-Hajjaj, who said: 
I asked Abu Abdillah {a.s} about the excess that may result from measuring or weighing. 
Imam {a.s} said: "If there is no deliberate excess (beyond fairness), then there is no 
harm." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.5 p.182 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.210 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.40 
• Al-Wafi, Vol.17 p.477 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.87 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 68, H. 3783
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٨، ح ٣٧٨٣

hadiths.ar: 3785  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ج َّمِي ل  عَّن ْ ر ُّرَّإرَّة َّ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ إِس ْي َّزَّى مِن ْ طَّعَّامِ ق َّزْي ه ٍ ن ِعَّي ْن ِهِ ق َّق َّالَّ
hadiths.en:
Jamil asked Abu Abdillah {a.s} about a man who buys straw from a threshing 
floor, each load (kurr) for a specified price, and takes possession of the straw but sells 
it before measuring the grain. 
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm in that." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.210 
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
«

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 68, H. 3784
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٨، ح ٣٧٨٤

hadiths.ar:
3786   -  وَّ رَّوَّى إِن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  عُّمَّي ْزٍ عَّن ِ إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ ن ْن ِ عَّطِي  َّه َّ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ع ق ُّلْث ُّ إ ِن  َّا ن َّس ْي َّزِى  إَّلط َّعَّامَّ مِن َّ إَّلس ُّق ُّن ِ  
ي ُّم َّ ن َّكِي لُّهُّ ق َّي َّز ِي ذُّ ق َّال لْث ُّ لأَّ ق َّال
hadiths.en:
Jamil narrated from Zurara, who said:  
I asked Abu Ja’far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} about a man who purchased 
food specifically from a village. 
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm in that. If the food is delivered, it belongs to him. But 
if it is not delivered, then it remains a debt upon the seller."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.211 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.7 p.39 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 
p.562 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.313 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 68, H. 3785
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٨، ح ٣٧٨٥

hadiths.ar:
3787   -  ورَّوَّى جَّم َّاذ  عَّن ِ إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى ِّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّس ْي َّزِى  إَّلي  َّمَّزَّة َّ ي ُّم َّ  ي َّن ِي عُّهَّا ق َّن ْلَّ إ َّن ْ 
ي َّا ْج ُّذ َّهَّا ق َّال اَّمُّ عَّن ْ س ِزَّإءِ إَّلن  َّح ْلِ وَّ إَّلْكَّزْمِ وَّ إَّلي ِّمَّارِ ي َّلاَّب َّ  
سِن ِي ن َّ وَّ إ َّرْن َّغَّ ق َّال ي َّزَّي ْي َّهُّ سَّن َّه ً وَّإجِذَّة ً ق َّلاَّ 
ي َّس ْي َّزِةِ جَّي  َّى ي َّن ْلُّغ  ق َّالَّ وَّ سُّن ِلَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّس ْي َّزِى  إَّلي  َّمَّزَّة َّ إَّلْمُّسَّم َّاة َّ مِن َّ إَّلْأ َّرْض ِ ق َّي َّهْلِك ُّ ي َّمَّزَّة ُّ  ي ِلْك َّ 
إَّلْأ َّرْض ِ كُّل ُّهَّا ق َّق َّال وإ ي َّذ ْكُّزُّون َّ ذ َّلِك َّ ق َّلَّم َّا رَّإ هُّمْ لأَّ 
ي َّذَّعُّون َّ إَّلْح ُّصُّومَّه َّ ن َّهَّاهُّمْ عَّن ْ ذ َّلِك َّ إَّلْن َّي ْغِ جَّي  َّى ي َّن ْلُّغ َّ إَّلي  َّمَّزَّة ُّ وَّ لَّمْ ي ُّحَّزِّمْهُّ وَّ لَّكِن ْ ق َّعَّلَّ ذ َّ لِك َّ مِن ْ إ َّج ْلِ ج ُّصُّومَّي ِهِمْ
hadiths.en:
Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Al-Hasan ibn Atiyyah, who said: 
I asked Abu Abdillah {a.s}: "We buy food from ships, and then we measure it, sometimes 
finding an excess." 
Imam {a.s} said: "And perhaps it might sometimes be less for you?"  
I said: "Yes." 
Imam {a.s} said: "If it is less, do they compensate you?" 
I said: "No." 
Imam {a.s} said: "Then there is no harm." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.5 p.182 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.211 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.39 
• Al-Wafi, Vol.17 p.477 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.87 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 68, H. 3786
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٨، ح ٣٧٨٦

hadiths.ar:
3788  -  وَّ رَّوَّى جَّم َّاذُّ ن ْن ُّ عِي سَّى عَّن ْ رِن ْعِى ٍّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّن ِي غُّ إَّلي  َّمَّزَّة َّ ي ُّم َّ ي َّسْي َّي ْن ِى  
كَّي ْلاً وَّ ي َّمْزإً ق َّال  وَّ ي َّسْي َّي ْن ِى  إ َّوْسَّاق اً ي َّعْن ِى  إ َّن َّا 
عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّ الَّ ق َّن َّط َّزَّ إ ِلَّي ْهِ وَّ لَّمْ ي ُّن ْكِزْ ذ َّلِك َّ مِن ْ ق َّوْلِهِ.
hadiths.en:
Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi, who reported from Abu Abdillah {a.s}:  
I asked him about a man who buys fruits and then sells them before taking possession 
of them. 
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm if he finds profit in them; let him sell."  
Imam {a.s} was asked about purchasing palm trees, grapevines, and fruits for three or 
four years. 
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm in it, as you may say, 'If it does not yield in this year, it 
will yield in the next.' But if you buy it for one year, do not purchase it until the fruit has 
matured." 
Imam {a.s} was also asked about a man who buys specific fruits from a piece of land, 
and then all the fruits of that land are destroyed. 
Imam {a.s} said: "They disputed this matter before the Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace be 
upon him and his family), and they would continue to argue about it. So when he saw 
that they would not stop disputing, he forbade that sale until the fruit matures. 
However, he did not prohibit it entirely; rather, he forbade it due to their di sputes." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.211 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 68, H. 3787
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٨، ح ٣٧٨٧

hadiths.ar:
3789  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ر ُّرْعَّه ُّ عَّن ْ سَّمَّاعَّه َّ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ْ ن َّي ْغِ إَّلي  َّمَّزَّة ِ هَّلْ ي َّصْلُّحُّ س ِزَّإو ُّهَّا ق َّن ْلَّ إ َّن ْ ي َّح ْزُّح َّ ط لْعُّهَّا ق َّق َّال 
إ ِلأ َّ إ َّن ْ ي َّس ْي َّزِى َّ مَّعَّهَّا س َّي ْن اً مِن ْ ع َّي ْزِهَّا رَّطْن َّه ً إ َّوْ ن َّق ْلَّه ً ق َّي َّق ُّولُّ إ َّس ْي َّزِى  مِن ْك َّ هَّذ ِ ةِ إَّلز َّطْن َّه َّ وَّ هَّذ َّإ إَّلن  َّح ْلَّ وَّ هَّذ َّإ إَّلس  َّح َّزَّ 
ن ِكَّذ َّإ وَّ كَّذ َّإ ق َّا ِن ْ لَّمْ ي َّح ْزُّح ِ إَّلي  َّمَّزَّة ُّ كَّان َّ رَّإ ْسُّ مَّالِ إَّلْمُّس ْي َّزِى  ق ِى  إَّلز َّطْن َّه ِ وَّ إَّلْن َّق ْلِ سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ْ وَّرَّق ِ إَّلس  َّح َّزِ هَّلْ 
ي َّصْلُّحُّ س ِزَّإو ُّةُّ ي َّلاَّب َّ ج َّزَّ طَّاب ٍ إ َّوْ إ َّرْن َّغَّ ج َّزَّطَّاب ٍ ق َّق َّال
hadiths.en:
Hammad ibn Isa narrated from Ribi, who reported from Abu Abdillah {a.s}: 
Regarding a man who sells fruits and then excludes a specific measure (a portion) of 
dates, 
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm in it." 
It was mentioned that one of his servants was sitting with him, and the servant said:  
"He indeed sells and excludes certain measures (Awsaq)" - referring to Abu Abdillah 
{a.s}. 
Imam {a.s} then looked at him and did not disapprove of what he had said.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.211 • Al-Wafi, Vol.17 p.535 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.18 
p.242 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 68, H. 3788
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٨، ح ٣٧٨٨

hadiths.ar:
3790  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْق َّاسِمُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّحَّم َّذٍ عَّن ْ عَّلِى ِّ ن ْن ِ إ َّن ِى  جَّمْز َّة َّ ق َّال سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ إِس ْي َّزَّى  
ن ُّسْي َّان اً ق ِي هِ ن َّح ْل  وَّ س َّح َّز  مِن ْهُّ مَّا ق َّذْ إ َّطْعَّمَّ وَّ مِن ْهُّ مَّا لَّمْ ي ُّطْعِمْ ق َّال هِ مَّا ق َّذْ إ َّطْعَّم
hadiths.en:
Zur'ah narrated from Sama'ah, who said: 
I asked him about selling fruits - whether it is permissible to buy them before their buds 
appear. 
Imam {a.s} said: "No, except if he buys something along with it, like fresh greens or 
vegetables, and says: 'I am buying these greens, this palm tree, and this tree for such 
and such price.' 
If the fruit does not emerge, the buyer’s capital will be in the greens and vegetables."  
Imam {a.s} was also asked about buying tree leaves, whether it is permissible to buy 
them for three or four harvests. 
Imam {a.s} said: "If you see the leaves on the tree, then buy as many harvests as you 
wish." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.5 p.176 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.212 • Al-Wafi, Vol.17 p.536 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 68, H. 3789
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٨، ح ٣٧٨٩

hadiths.ar:
3791  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ِ إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ ن ْن ِ عَّلِى ِّ ن ْن ِ ن ِن ْث ِ إ ِلْي َّاسَّ ق َّالَّ : ق ُّلْث ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ هَّلْ ي َّح ُّور ُّ ن َّي ْغُّ إَّلن  َّح ْلِ  
إ ِذ َّإ جَّمَّلَّ ق َّال ز
hadiths.en:
Al-Qasim bin Muhammad narrated from Ali bin Abi Hamzah, who said:  
I asked Abu Abdillah {a.s} about a man who bought an orchard that contained palm trees 
and other trees, some of which had already borne fruit and others had not yet borne 
fruit. 
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm in it if there are trees that have already borne fruit."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.212 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.218 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 68, H. 3790
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٨، ح ٣٧٩٠

hadiths.ar:
3792  -  رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ ي َّعْق ُّوب َّ ن ْن ِ س ُّعَّي ْث ٍ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق ُّلْث ُّ إ ُّعْطِى  إَّلز َّج ُّل إَّلي  َّمَّن َّ عِس ْزِي ن َّ  
ذِي ن َّارإً وَّ إ َّق ُّولُّ لَّهُّ إ ِذ َّإ ق َّامَّث ْ ي َّمَّزَّي ُّك َّ ن ِس َّى ْءٍ ق َّهِى َّ لِى  ن ِذ َّلِك َّ إَّلي  َّمَّن ِ إ ِن ْ رَّص ِي ث َّ إ َّج َّذ ْب ُّ وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّزِ هْث َّ ي َّزَّكْث ُّ ق َّق َّال 
ي َّسْي َّطِي غُّ إ َّن ْ ي ُّعْطِي َّهُّ وَّ لأَّ ي َّس ْي َّزِطَّ س َّي ْن اً  لأَّ ي ُّسَّمِّى  س َّي ْن اً وَّ إَّلل َّهُّ ي َّعْلَّمُّ مِن ْ ن ِي  َّي ِهِ ذ َّلِك َّ ق َّال 
ي َّصْلُّحُّ إ ِذ َّإ كَّان َّ مِن ْ ن ِي  َّي ِهِ ذ َّلِك
hadiths.en:
Al-Hasan bin Ali bin Bint Ilyas narrated: 
I asked Abu Al -Hasan {a.s}: Is it permissible to sell palm trees when they have borne 
fruit? 
Imam {a.s} said: "It is not permissible to sell them until they ripen."  
I said: What is meant by ripening, may I be your ransom?  
Imam {a.s} said: "When they turn red or yellow." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.212 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 68, H. 3791
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٨، ح ٣٧٩١

hadiths.ar:
3793  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّاصِمُّ ن ْن ُّ جُّمَّي ْذٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ن َّصِي زٍ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ   ي َّق ُّولُّ لِلز َّج ُّلِ 
إ َّن ْي َّاعُّ لَّك َّ مَّي َّاعاً وَّ إَّلزِّن ْحُّ ن َّي ْن ِى  وَّ ن َّي ْن َّك َّ ق َّال
hadiths.en:
Ya'qub bin Shu'ayb narrated: 
I asked Abu Abdullah {a.s}: I give a man twenty dinars and say to him, "If your fruit 
reaches a certain value, it is mine for that price. If I agree, I will take it, and if I dislike it, 
I will leave it." 
Imam {a.s} said: "Can you not give it to him without making any condition?"  
I said: May I be your ransom! What if he does not specify anything, but Allah {SWT} knows 
his intention? 
Imam {a.s} said: "It is not permissible if that is his intention." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.5 p.176 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.212 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.89 
• Al-Wafi, Vol.17 p.537 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.221 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 68, H. 3792
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٨، ح ٣٧٩٢

hadiths.ar:
3794  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ مُّي َّسِّزٍ ن َّي  َّاعِ إَّلز  ُّطِّى ِّ ق َّالَّ : ق ُّلْث ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ ِن  َّا ن َّس ْي َّزِى  إَّلْمَّي َّاعَّ ن ِن َّ ط ِزَّة ٍ ق َّي َّح ِى ءُّ 
إَّلز َّج ُّلُّ ق َّي َّق ُّولُّ ن ِكَّمْ ي َّق َّو َّمَّ عَّلَّي ْك َّ ق َّا َّق ُّولُّ ي ُّق َّو َّمُّ ن ِكَّذ َّإ وَّ كَّذ َّإ ق َّا َّن ِي عُّهُّ ن ِزِن ْحٍ ق َّال ذ َّإ ن ِعْي َّهُّ مُّزَّإن َّحَّه ً كَّان َّ لَّهُّ مِن َّ إَّلن  َّط ِزَّة ِ 
مِي ْلُّ مَّا لَّك هُّ مُّزَّإن َّحَّه ً ق َّي ُّس ْي َّزَّى مِن ِّى   
وَّ لَّوْ وُّص ِعْث ُّ مِن ْ رَّإ ْسِ إَّلْمَّالِ جَّي  َّ ى إ َّق ُّولَّ ي ُّق َّو َّمُّ ن ِكَّذ َّإ وَّ كَّذ َّإ ق َّالَّ ق َّلَّم َّا رَّإ َّى مَّا س َّق  َّ عَّلَّى  َّ ق َّال 
ي َّكُّون ُّ لَّك َّ ق ِي هِ ق َّزَّح  ن ِزِن ْحٍ
hadiths.en:
Asim bin Humayd narrated from Abu Basir: 
I asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about a man who says to another man, "I will buy 
merchandise for you, and the profit will be shared between me and you."  
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm in it." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.213 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.7 p.56 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 
p.695 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.75 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.20 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 68, H. 3793
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٨، ح ٣٧٩٣

hadiths.ar:
3795  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ عَّن ْذِ إَّلز َّجْمَّن ِ ن ْن ِ إَّلْحَّح  َّاح ِ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي ق ُّولُّ لَّهُّ إَّلز َّج ُّلُّ  
إ َّس ْي َّزِى  مِن ْك َّ إَّلْمَّي َّاعَّ عَّلَّى إ َّن ْ  ي َّح ْعَّلَّ لِى  ق ِى  كُّلِّ ي َّوْب ٍ إ َّس ْي َّزِي هِ مِن ْك َّ كَّذ َّإ وَّ كَّذ َّإ وَّ إ ِن  َّمَّا ي َّس ْي َّزِى  لِلن  َّاسِ وَّ ي َّق ُّولُّ إِج ْعَّلْ  
لِى  رِن ْحاً عَّلَّى إ َّن ْ إ َّس ْي َّزِى َّ مِن ْك َّ ق َّكَّزِهَّهُّ.
hadiths.en:
Maysir, the seller of fabrics, narrated: 
I said to Abu Abdullah {a.s}: We purchase merchandise on credit, and a man comes and 
asks, "For how much was it valued for you?" I reply, "It was valued at such and such," 
and then I sell it to him at a profit. 
Imam {a.s} said: "If you sell it on a profit basis (Murabaha), then he is entitled to the same 
credit period as you." 
I became distressed and said: “We are ruined!" 
Imam {a.s} asked: “Why?" 
I said: “Because the goods I sell on a Murabaha basis are purchased from me, and if I 
deduct from the principal amount to state its valuation, it would cause difficulty."  
When Imam {a.s} saw what troubled me, Imam {a.s} said: "Shall I not open for you a door 
that will bring you ease?" 
I said: “Indeed, please do!" 
Imam {a.s} said: "Say, 'It cost me such and such, and I sell it to you for such and such,' 
and do not mention the word 'profit' (Ribh)." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol. 5 p.198 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 3 p.213 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 18 p.689 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.82 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 68, H. 3794
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٨، ح ٣٧٩٤

hadiths.ar:
3796  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ ن َّس  َّارِ ن ْن ِ ي َّسَّارٍ ق َّال سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّ عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّن ِي غُّ إَّلْمَّي َّاعَّ ن ِن َّسَّاءٍ إ َّ ي َّس ْي َّزِي هِ مِن ْ صَّاجِن ِهِ 
إَّل َّذ ِى  ي َّن ِي عُّهُّ مِن ْهُّ ق َّال لأَّ ن َّق َّزَّك َّ وَّ لأَّ ع َّ ن َّمَّك
hadiths.en:
Abdul-Rahman ibn Al-Hajjaj narrated: I asked Abu Al-Hasan {a.s} about a man 
who said to another man: "I will buy merchandise from you, provided that you give me 
such and such profit on each piece of cloth I purchase from you."  
The man, however, buys the merchandise for others and says,  
"Grant me a profit because I am buying from you." 
Imam {a.s} disliked this arrangement. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.214 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 p.696 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.18 
p.58 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.76 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 68, H. 3795
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٨، ح ٣٧٩٥

hadiths.ar:
3797  -  وَّ رَّوَّى جَّم َّاذ  عَّن ِ إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى ِّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : إ َّن  َّه سُّن ِلَّ عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّن ْي َّاعُّ إَّلي  َّوْب َّ مِن َّ إَّلس ُّوق ِ 
لِأ َّهْلِهِ وَّ ي َّا ْج ُّذ ُّةُّ ن ِس َّزْطٍ ق َّي ُّعْطَّى إَّلزِّن ْحَّ ق ِى  إ َّهْلِهِ ق َّال عَّلَّى ن َّق ْسِهِ وَّ لأَّ ي َّح ْعَّلْ 
ق ِى  ن َّق ْسِهِ إ َّن ْ ي َّزُّذ َّ إَّلي  َّوْب َّ عَّلَّ ى صَّاجِن ِهِ إ ِن ْ رُّذ َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ
hadiths.en:
Bashshar ibn Yasar narrated: I asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about a man who sells 
merchandise for deferred payment (on credit).  
Can he buy it back from the one to whom he sold it? 
Imam {a.s} replied: "Yes, there is no problem with it." 
I then asked him: "Can I buy back my own merchandise?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "It is not your merchandise, nor your cattle, nor your sheep."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.5 p.208 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.214 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.47 
• Al-Wafi, Vol.18 p.715 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.41 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 68, H. 3796
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٨، ح ٣٧٩٦

hadiths.ar:
3798 - وَّ رَّوَّى إِن ْن ُّ مُّسْكَّان َّ عَّن ْ عِي سَّى ن ْن ِ إ َّن ِى  مَّن ْصُّورٍ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ِ إ لْق َّوْمِ ي َّس ْي َّزُّون َّ 
إَّلْح ِزَّإب َّ إَّلْهَّزَّوِى  َّ إ َّوِ  إَّلْكَّزَّوِى  َّ إ َّوِ إَّلْمَّزْوَّر ِى  َّ إ َّوِ إَّلْق ُّوهِى  َّ ق َّي َّس ْي َّزِى  إَّلز َّج ُّلُّ مِن ْهُّمْ عَّس َّزَّة َّ إ َّي ْوَّإب ٍ ي َّس ْي َّزِطُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ ج ِ ي َّارَّةُّ كُّل ُّ  
ي َّوْب ٍ ج َّمْسَّه ُّ ذَّرَّإهِمَّ إ َّوْ إ َّق َّل ُّ إ َّوْ إ َّكْي َّزُّ ق َّق َّال ذْ ق ِي هِ ج ِي َّارإً ع َّي ْزَّ ج َّمْسَّه ِ إ َّي ْوَّإب ٍ وَّ 
وَّج َّذَّ ن َّق ِي  َّي َّهُّ سَّوَّإءً زَّة َّ إ َّي ْوَّإب ٍ ق َّزَّذ َّذَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ مِزَّإرإً 
ق َّق َّالَّ إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّ لس َّلاَّم ق ِي  َّي َّهُّ 
سَّوَّإءً
hadiths.en:
Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi, who reported from Abu Abdullah {a.s}: 
He was asked about a man who buys a garment from the market for his family and 
takes it with a condition that he may return it if not accepted, but he is offered a profit 
if his family keeps it. 
Imam {a.s} said: "If he desires the profit, then he must make the garment binding upon 
himself (as a confirmed purchase) and should not intend in his heart to return the 
garment to its owner if it is returned to him." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.214 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.25 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 68, H. 3797
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٨، ح ٣٧٩٧

hadiths.ar:
3799   -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن ُّو إَّلص َّن  َّاحِ إَّلْكِن َّان ِى  ُّ وَّ سَّمَّاعَّه ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : إ َّن  َّهُّ سُّن ِلَّ عَّن ِ   إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّحْمِلُّ إَّلْمَّي َّاعَّ  
لِأ َّهْلِ إَّلس ُّوق ِ وَّ ق َّذْ ق َّو َّمُّوإ عَّلَّي ْهِ  ق ِي مَّه ً ق َّي َّق ُّولُّون َّ ن ِغْ ق َّمَّا إِر ْذَّذْب َّ ق َّلَّك َّ ق َّال
hadiths.en:
Ibn Muskan narrated from Isa ibn Abi Mansur, who said:  
I asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about a group of people who buy Herawi, Karawi, Marwazi, or 
Quhi bundles of clothes. A man among them purchases ten garments, stipulating a 
condition that each garment is worth five dirhams, or less, or more.  
Imam {a.s} said: "I do not like this type of sale. What if he does not find any choice among 
them except five garments, and the rest are all the same?"  
Then Isma'il, his son, said to him: "They have stipulated that he must take ten 
garments from them." 
Abu Abdullah {a.s} repeatedly responded: "He has only stipulated with them to take the 
best among them. What if he only finds five that are suitable, and the rest are all the 
same?" 
Then Imam {a.s} said: "I do not like this type of sale." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.215 
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
»

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 68, H. 3798
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٨، ح ٣٧٩٨

hadiths.ar:
3800  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عُّن َّي ْذُّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ُّ عَّلِى ٍّ إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى  ُّ وَّ مُّحَّم َّذ  إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلا مُّ ق َّالَّ : ق ُّذِّمَّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ  
إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ  مَّي َّاع  مِن ْ مِصْزٍ ق َّصَّن َّغَّ طَّعَّاماً وَّ ذَّعَّا لَّهُّ إَّلي  ُّح  َّارَّ ق َّق َّالُّوإ ن َّا ْج ُّذ ُّةُّ ن ِذَّةْ ذَّوَّإر ْذَّةْ ق َّق َّال ون ُّ ذ َّلِك 
ق َّق َّالُّوإ ق ِى  كُّلِّ عَّس َّزَّة ِ إ لأَّف ٍ إ َّلْق َّي ْن ِ ق َّال »
hadiths.en:
Abu al -Sabbah al -Kinani and Sama’ah narrated  from Abu Abdullah  Imam 
Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}: 
Imam {a.s} was asked about a man who carries goods for the people of the market, and 
they have set a price for him. They then say to him, “Sell it, and whatever profit you 
make above the price is yours.” 
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm in that, but he should not sell it to them as a murabaha 
(profit-sharing agreement)." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.5 p.195 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.215 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.54 
• Al-Wafi, Vol.18 p.674 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.57 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 68, H. 3799
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٨، ح ٣٧٩٩

hadiths.ar:
3801  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْعَّلاَّءُّ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْلِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّجَّذِهِمَّا عَّ : ق ِى إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّس ْي َّزِى  إَّلْمَّي َّاعَّ ج َّمِي عاً ن ِي َّمَّن ٍ ي ُّم َّ ي ُّق َّوِّمُّ كُّل َّ 
ي َّوْب ٍ ن ِمَّا ي َّسْوَّى جَّي  َّى ي َّق َّغَّ عَّلَّى رَّإ ْسِ مَّالِهِ ي َّن ِي عُّهُّ مُّزَّإن َّحَّه ً ي َّوْن اً ي َّوْن اً ق َّال  إ َّن  َّهُّ إ ِن  َّمَّا ق َّو َّمَّه
hadiths.en:
Ubaydullah ibn Ali al -Halabi and Muhammad al -Halabi narrated  from Abu 
Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}: 
Goods arrived for Abu Abdullah {a.s} from Egypt. Imam {a.s} prepared food and invited the 
merchants.  
They said: “We will take it at dah dawazdah (a price of twelve).” 
Imam {a.s} asked: “And how much does that amount to?” 
They replied: “For every ten thousand, two thousand more.”  
Imam {a.s} said: “Then I will sell you these goods for twelve thousand.” 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.216 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.7 p.54 • Awali Al -La’ali, 
Vol.3 p.214 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 68, H. 3800
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٨، ح ٣٨٠٠

hadiths.ar:
3802  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ عُّمَّزَّ ن ْن ِ ي َّز ِي ذ ق َّالَّ : ن ِعْث ُّ ن ِالْمَّذِي ن َّه ِ ج ِزَّإن اً هَّزَّوِي  اً كُّل َّ ي َّوْب ٍ ن ِكَّذ َّإ وَّ كَّذ َّإ ق َّا َّج َّذ ُّوةُّ ق َّاق ْي َّسَّمُّوةُّ ي ُّم َّ 
وَّج َّذُّوإ ن ِي َّوْب ٍ ق ِي هَّا عَّي ْن اً ق َّزَّذ ُّوةُّ عَّلَّى  َّ ق َّق ُّلْث ُّ لَّهُّمْ إ ُّعْطِي كُّمْ ي َّمَّن َّهُّ إَّل َّذ ِى  ن ِعْي ُّكُّمْ ن ِهِ ق َّق َّالُّوإ ل أَّ وَّ لَّكِن  َّا ن َّا ْ ج ُّذ ُّ ق ِي مَّي َّهُّ مِن ْك َّ 
ق َّذ َّكَّزْب ُّ ذ َّلِك َّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّق َّال
hadiths.en:
Al-'Ala narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from one of the Imams {a.s}, 
regarding a man who buys goods altogether for a set price and then appraises each 
garment based on its value until it equals his capital, selling it item by item at a profit  
Imam {a.s} said: "No, not until he clarifies to the buyer that he merely appraised it."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.5 p.197 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, V.3 p.216 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, V.7 p.55 • 
Al-Wafi, V.18 p.685 • Al-Wafi, V.18 p.685 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.77 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.78 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 68, H. 3801
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٨، ح ٣٨٠١

hadiths.ar:
3803   -  وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ ج َّمِي لِ ن ْن ِ ذَّر َّإح ٍ عَّن ْ ن َّعْض ِ إ َّصْحَّان ِن َّا عَّن ْ إ َّجَّذِهِمَّا عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّس ْ ي َّزِى  إَّلي  َّوْب َّ 
مِن َّ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ إ َّوِ إَّلْمَّي َّاعَّ ق َّي َّح ِذُّ ن ِهِ عَّي ْن اً ق َّال ن ِعَّي ْن ِهِ رَّذ َّةُّ عَّلَّى صَّاجِن ِهِ وَّ إ َّج َّذ َّ إَّلي  َّمَّن َّ وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ ج َّاطَّ  
إَّلي  َّوْب َّ إ َّوْ صَّن َّع َّهُّ إ َّوْ ق َّطَّعَّهُّ رَّج َّغَّ ن ِن ُّق ْصَّان ِ إَّلْعَّي ْث ِ
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Umar ibn Yazid:  I sold a Harawi sack in Medina, with each 
garment priced at a specific amount. They took it and divided it among them. Then they 
found a defect in one of the garments and returned it to me.  
I said to them, "I will give you its price that I sold it to you for."  
They replied: “No, rather we will take its value from you."  
I mentioned this matter to Abu Abdullah {a.s},  
Imam {a.s} said: "They are obligated to that." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.216 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 p.735 
 ﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 68, H. 3802
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٨، ح ٣٨٠٢
